statement: Michael Bleich weaponized the legal system of the State of New York to harass his neighbor, obstruct legitimate building maintenance, obtain a fraudulent court order through perjured testimony, tamper with physical evidence to deceive regulatory inspectors, and ultimately demand $6 to $8 million for a condominium unit independently appraised at $2.5 million
legal_significance: Introduction - narrative regarding Bleich's conduct

statement: The disparity between the two units is the foundational fact of every dispute that followed
legal_significance: Unit 1 vs Unit 2 structural and legal differences

statement: Unit 1 occupies approximately 2,900 square feet, more than half of which is below grade—a cellar that the building's Certificate of Occupancy (C of O No. 107294, issued 1995) restricts to 'Recreation Room, Tenant Storage, Meters.' Not bedrooms. Not living space. Storage.
asserted_in: Certificate of Occupancy C of O No. 107294, issued 1995
legal_significance: Legal use restrictions on Unit 1

statement: The Paltrow-Martin transaction established 13 Harrison Street as a celebrity-grade address. The building had housed an Oscar winner, a Grammy-winning rock star, and their famous friends—and it had nearly doubled in value in two years.
legal_significance: Property value appreciation 2005-2007

statement: Bleich's demand of $6–8 million for a cellar duplex sits in the shadow of Taylor Swift's $50 million compound. The neighborhood's extraordinary real estate values underscore both the potential of the Harrison Street building—if properly maintained and renovated—and the destructive absurdity of Bleich's campaign to prevent that renovation through fraudulent litigation.
legal_significance: Characterization of Bleich's conduct as fraudulent

statement: Today, the building is not appreciating. It is deteriorating. The temporary roof is failing. Water pours through 299 documented failure points. The kitchen of Unit 2 has been destroyed by mold. The fourth floor is uninhabitable. The construction loan has defaulted. And the man responsible for this destruction—Michael Bleich, the owner of a cellar duplex that an independent appraiser valued at $2.5 million—is demanding $6 to $8 million to go away.
legal_significance: Current condition of building and Bleich's demands

statement: The $6.5 million figure was 'the price that the seller wanted to list the apartment for.'
legal_significance: Bleich's initial listing price for Unit 1 in 2017

statement: The unit was 'half basement.'
legal_significance: Assessment of Unit 1's condition and marketability

statement: The cellar rooms were not legal bedrooms under the Certificate of Occupancy and that Brown Harris Stevens could not market them as bedrooms.
asserted_in: conversation with Bleich and later testimony
legal_significance: Legal characterization of Unit 1's basement space

statement: Bleich became angry, demanded cancellation papers, and fired him.
legal_significance: Bleich's reaction to Kornmeier's assessment of Unit 1

statement: Morgan Brill and Michael Bleich were elected as co-Presidents of the condominium board.
legal_significance: Board election recorded in meeting minutes

statement: Alexis Bleich was elected as Secretary.
legal_significance: Board election recorded in meeting minutes

statement: Michael and Alexis Bleich were named as Resident Managers.
legal_significance: Board appointments recorded in meeting minutes

statement: All other business—budget, building operations—was tabled for the 'next meeting,' timing to be set by 'remaining board officers and new owner of Unit 2.'
legal_significance: Tabling of business items in meeting minutes

statement: Under the condominium's By-Laws (Article VIII, Section 5), the Right of First Refusal gave the Board—and by extension, the majority interest holder—the right to purchase any unit before it could be sold to a third party.
legal_significance: Legal significance of ROFR provisions

statement: Waiving the ROFR through December 2019 meant that Bleich could sell Unit 1 to anyone, at any price, without giving the 75% owner the opportunity to acquire the minority stake and consolidate the building.
legal_significance: Consequences of the ROFR waiver

statement: The By-Laws further provide (Article XI) that amendments 'which adversely affects the Owner of the Unit(s) is not valid without such Owner's approval.'
legal_significance: Legal requirement for amendment approval

statement: The 75% owner never approved the ROFR waiver. He was not even told it existed until after the closing.
legal_significance: Allegation of concealment of ROFR waiver from majority owner

statement: We never had an in-person board meeting. We just talked on the phone.
legal_significance: Contradiction of the October 15, 2018 board meeting narrative

statement: We only ever met maybe twice. Was by phone and I didn't take much notes. If any. Definitely don't still have them. Sorry.
legal_significance: Prior board governance and lack of documentation

statement: I do see some notes from you/your attorney regarding some ROFR I supposedly signed/granted. I can assure you that never happened. I have ROFR's myself on several properties abutting various residences of mine, so I am quite familiar with them and there was none of that happening at 13 Harrison.
legal_significance: Denial of ROFR waiver discussion, negotiation, or execution

statement: Mike was always distant, if inconsiderate and unhelpful (never shoveled snow, cleaned the sidewalk, paid the tickets for having a dirty sidewalk, watered the plants outside or did anything to help take care of the shared vestibule).
legal_significance: Character assessment and behavior during shared ownership

statement: He thought his duplex PH apartment was comparable value-wise to a 2-story garden townhouse in Carroll Gardens/Cobble Hill and at that point I decided he was pretty much delusional.
legal_significance: Characterization of Bleich's valuation beliefs

statement: This is definitely not true. We talked about selling it together hypothetically but we never had any 'agreement.'
legal_significance: Denial of binding 60/40 revenue split agreement

statement: His continued insistence on a 60/40 split—which goes against the 25/75 split of the condo documents—was my primary reason to just cut my losses and get the hell out of Dodge.
legal_significance: Reason for selling unit—Bleich's inconsistent revenue sharing demands

statement: This discussion was always purely hypothetical because there was never anyone who gave a serious offer for the whole building.
legal_significance: Characterization of joint sale discussions

statement: Mike put his HVAC on the coping wall outside the window of the pink bedroom. There was no easement granted, because I guess we assumed there already was one, given that Apt 2 already had a unit up.
legal_significance: HVAC equipment placement and lack of formal easement grant

statement: The unit was probably a holdover from when the whole building was owned by the Kerzners (they lived upstairs and used the Bleich's space as an office).
legal_significance: Historical context of HVAC equipment installation

statement: We replaced all the HVAC units in 2008. I did a little work later when the house was on the market, just replacing the air handler for the pink bedroom.
legal_significance: HVAC maintenance and replacement history

statement: He just has a completely bonkers idea about the value of that place that is in no way linked to reality.
legal_significance: Assessment of Bleich's valuation approach

statement: It's unfortunate that he has managed to convince the judicial system that he's some sort of victim here. But he seems to have been pretty successful.
legal_significance: Assessment of Bleich's judicial success

statement: It seems like he's lying about a lot of things!! What is wrong with him?!
legal_significance: Assessment of Bleich's truthfulness

statement: Brill states she resigned 'as of yesterday'—meaning her resignation took effect on October 18, 2018, the very day of the Unit 2 closing.
legal_significance: Timing of Brill's board resignation

statement: On October 15, 2018—the date of the purported board meeting—Morgan Brill was still a sitting board member and co-President.
legal_significance: Brill's board status at time of October 15 meeting

statement: Brill was never notified of the October 15 meeting. She did not attend it in person. She did not vote on the ROFR waiver. She did not authorize it.
legal_significance: Brill's non-participation in October 15 meeting

statement: Under the By-Laws (Article IV, Section 7), the Board of Managers consists of two members.
legal_significance: Board composition requirement

statement: Without Brill's participation, there was no quorum. Without a quorum, no valid board action could be taken. The ROFR waiver was void from inception.
legal_significance: Legal consequence of lack of quorum for October 15 meeting

statement: Charles Berry argued that Brill had 'resigned as Co-President, leaving Michael as the sole President' upon the closing of Unit 2.
legal_significance: Justification for board composition

statement: Berry's own timeline was contradicted by the documentary record—Brill's resignation email was dated October 19, not October 15, and Brill herself confirmed the October 18 effective date.
legal_significance: Inconsistency in Berry's argument versus documentary evidence

statement: Bleich moved to consolidate control. The email record reveals a systematic effort to block the new 75% owner from exercising any governance rights.
legal_significance: Pattern of governance obstruction post-closing

statement: I don't have anything pressing to discuss prior to our meeting.
legal_significance: Claim of no urgent governance matters

statement: This is a 25% owner telling the 75% owner that there is nothing important to discuss about building governance.
legal_significance: Characterization of Bleich's November 1 statement

statement: His broker has a buyer with a budget of $15 million interested in the entire building for a private club.
legal_significance: Potential building sale opportunity

statement: This was the first of many communications in which Bleich would attempt to engineer a building sale on terms favorable to himself.
legal_significance: Pattern of Bleich's building sale efforts

statement: I am busy hosting thanksgiving today. As I mentioned, there is an interested party, not an offer. Next step would be for me to put you in touch with agent for a showing of your unit. Up to you if you want to proceed.
legal_significance: Response deflecting formal meeting request

statement: I am not interested in having an official unit holders meeting as we just had one.
legal_significance: Refusal to hold formal unit holder meeting

statement: The 'meeting' Bleich referenced was the October 15 meeting—the very meeting that Morgan Brill says never took place as an in-person event, at which the new 75% owner was not present, was not invited, and had no representation.
legal_significance: Bleich's use of phantom October 15 meeting to block governance

statement: We specifically put off a lot of stuff till the 'next meeting with the new owner' so I don't know how he can say you don't need a meeting.
legal_significance: Corroboration that October 15 meeting contemplated follow-up with new owner

statement: Tom is going to do some light work on the skylight air conditioning and ventilation above our back room, so please don't be alarmed if you see him from your back windows.
legal_significance: Notice of rooftop HVAC work

statement: He is also going to look into a slow leak that is apparently coming from unit 2 into our master bathroom.
legal_significance: Report of water leak from Unit 2 to Unit 1

statement: He is also going to work on airconditioning of our ventilation system and some architectural planning around the possible conversion of our apartment into office or retail space.
legal_significance: Commercial conversion planning for Unit 1

statement: It acknowledges the existence of rooftop HVAC equipment that Bleich would later claim as his exclusive property in sworn court filings—the equipment that would become the central factual predicate for the 2024 injunction.
legal_significance: Significance of December 1 email regarding HVAC

statement: It openly discusses the 'possible conversion' of Unit 1 'into office or retail space'—a commercial conversion that Bleich would later deny under oath and that he would instead falsely attribute to the Unit 2 owner in verified court filings.
legal_significance: Significance of December 1 email regarding commercial conversion

statement: It references 'a slow leak that is apparently coming from unit 2'—establishing the water-damage narrative that Bleich would weaponize for years to come.
legal_significance: Significance of December 1 email regarding water damage

statement: If Tom finds that he needs access to the HVAC equipment on the roof we will be in touch. This acknowledges that the HVAC equipment is on the roof—which is a Limited Common Element designated for Unit 2's exclusive use and maintenance under the Declaration.
legal_significance: Acknowledgment of HVAC location and potential need for roof access

statement: There is a water leak coming from your unit into ours. This may have begun over the last few days—we were away for the holiday and noticed it after coming home.
legal_significance: Report of water leak from Unit 2 to Unit 1

statement: I have someone we can call to look for the source—Tom McCracken, who I believe Ron has been in touch with, has a lot of experience with the building.
legal_significance: Proposal to engage building expert for leak investigation

statement: Bleich was willing to engage cooperatively on building maintenance issues when it suited his interests—but would refuse similar cooperation when the Unit 2 owner needed roof access for repairs that would benefit the entire building.
legal_significance: Pattern of selective cooperation on maintenance issues

statement: As CEO and Managing Member of Scout Trading, LLC (CRD# 151990), a Delaware-formed broker-dealer registered from November 2009 to September 2014, Bleich oversaw a firm that FINRA and NASDAQ would fine $3 million for systematic violations of Regulation SHO
legal_significance: Scout Trading regulatory penalties

statement: From January 2010 through March 2012, Scout Trading routinely submitted 'naked' redemption orders to its clearing firm, Wedbush Securities, for leveraged ETFs—orders to redeem shares the firm did not own
legal_significance: Naked redemption order violations

statement: Scout Trading submitted at least 255 naked redemption orders across 11 ETFs, totaling more than 295 million shares
legal_significance: Scope of naked redemption violations

statement: The average market value of excess naked positions was approximately $23 million
legal_significance: Financial scale of violations

statement: FINRA characterized the strategy as 'profitable but impermissible'
legal_significance: Nature of Scout Trading strategy

statement: In the case of one ETF (Direxion Daily Financial Bear 3X, ticker FAZ), Scout Trading's illegal actions accounted for 59% of all market-wide failures to deliver
legal_significance: Market impact of Scout Trading violations

statement: This caused 14 ETFs to be placed on the SEC Threshold Securities List for over 250 consecutive settlement days
legal_significance: Systemic market consequences

statement: FINRA found that Scout Trading operated with 'near-total supervisory negligence'—no written supervisory procedures, no compliance infrastructure, no internal monitoring
legal_significance: Supervisory deficiencies at Scout Trading

statement: Total documented regulatory penalties across all enforcement actions exceeded $3.3 million
legal_significance: Scout Trading total penalties

legal_significance: Roof damage and water intrusion as negotiating leverage

statement: The Bleichs converted the cellar—designated by the C of O for "Recreation Room, Tenant Storage, Meters"—into bedrooms for their three children. This was not an inadvertent code violation.
legal_significance: Illegal cellar conversion maintained for approximately 17 years (1995-2018). Phase 1 of five-phase criminal scheme.

statement: Bleich's reaction—described by Kornmeier as "annoyed" and "aggressive"—demonstrates premeditated knowledge of the illegality.
legal_significance: When informed the converted cellar rooms were not legal bedrooms and could not be marketed as such, Bleich demonstrated premeditated knowledge of illegality.

statement: Bleich was explicitly informed of the cellar's illegal status by Kornmeier in 2017. His counsel Giacovas acknowledged the cellar bedroom use on the record in September 2022. Despite this documented knowledge, Bleich continued to represent to the court that the HVAC serving these illegal rooms was a "residential necessity."
legal_significance: Knowledge and concealment (Phase 2, 2017-2019). Fraudulent misrepresentation to court regarding HVAC necessity.

statement: On three documented occasions prior to 2024, the Bleichs refused the Department of Buildings entry to inspect Unit 1's cellar. In June 2024, they erected sheetrock and cardboard over the illegal bedroom and bathroom doors to conceal them from DOB inspectors.
asserted_by: Documented occasions and Department of Buildings records
legal_significance: Physical evidence tampering—deliberate alteration of premises to deceive regulatory body. Systematic obstruction (Phase 3, 2019-2024).

statement: Bleich swore that the rooftop HVAC was his family's sole heating source and that removal would cause irreparable harm. Every material element of this statement was false: the unit has a separate boiler and radiators; it has a rear-yard condenser; Bleich himself admitted he supplements with wall units; and the rooftop equipment was not even functional.
legal_significance: Perjured affirmation (Phase 4, June 2024). Multiple material misstatements regarding heating equipment, sole source claim, necessity, and functionality.

statement: Armed with an injunction obtained through perjury that was actively destroying his neighbor's property, Bleich demanded $6–8 million for a unit appraised at $2.5 million and formalized the demand through a Term Sheet that explicitly preserved the injunction as an enforcement weapon during any "settlement."
legal_significance: Extortion demand (Phase 5, May-September 2025). Demanding grossly excessive price ($6-8M vs. $2.5M appraisal) while using fraudulently obtained injunction as enforcement mechanism.

statement: Bleich treats legal boundaries as opportunities for arbitrage rather than constraints on conduct.
legal_significance: Structural pattern linking FINRA disciplinary conduct (255 fraudulent transactions, $3M in fines) to current litigation scheme (perjury, evidence tampering, extortion). Same behavioral pattern: exploit regulatory gaps, avoid compliance infrastructure, profit from deception.

statement: Fraud
legal_significance: Civil cause of action across four active proceedings. Bleich's false statements to court regarding HVAC necessity, heating source exclusivity, equipment functionality, and necessity for preservation.

statement: Perjury forming the basis for vacatur under CPLR §5015(a)(3)
legal_significance: Civil remedy—motion to vacate/modify injunction based on fraud in the inducement. Six proofs of perjury identified regarding HVAC statements.

statement: Breach of condominium governing documents
legal_significance: Civil cause of action. Illegal residential use of cellar in violation of Certificate of Occupancy designation and condominium by-laws.

statement: Private nuisance
legal_significance: Civil cause of action. Unauthorized rooftop HVAC equipment causing structural damage, water intrusion, mold, and ceiling deterioration to neighboring Unit 2.

statement: Trespass
legal_significance: Civil cause of action. Unauthorized installation and maintenance of HVAC equipment on Limited Common Element roof.

statement: Tortious interference with prospective economic advantage
legal_significance: Civil cause of action. Fraudulently obtained injunction prevented completion of Unit 2 renovation, destroying prospective economic advantage.

statement: Abuse of process
legal_significance: Civil cause of action. Use of injunction as leverage for extortionate settlement demands ($6-8M for $2.5M appraised unit).

statement: Conversion/unjust enrichment
legal_significance: Civil cause of action. Fifteen-plus years of unauthorized use of Limited Common Element roof for unpermitted HVAC equipment without license or compensation.

statement: Judiciary Law §487 (attorney deceit, treble damages)
legal_significance: Civil cause of action against attorney defendants (Giacovas, Felix, Judell). Dishonest conduct in presenting perjured affidavits and concealing illegal conditions from tribunal.

statement: Grand Larceny by Extortion (NYPL §155.05(2)(e))
legal_significance: Criminal charge. Demanding $6-8M while preserving fraudulently obtained injunction as enforcement weapon. Property value approximately $2.5M.

statement: Perjury in the First Degree (§210.15)
legal_significance: Criminal charge. Multiple false material statements in June 2024 affirmation regarding HVAC as sole heating source, necessity, functionality, and irreparable harm from removal.

statement: Tampering with Physical Evidence (§215.40)
legal_significance: Criminal charge. Erection of sheetrock and cardboard over illegal bedroom and bathroom doors in June 2024 to conceal from DOB inspectors.

statement: Obstruction of Governmental Administration (§195.05)
legal_significance: Criminal charge. Refusal of Department of Buildings entry on three documented occasions prior to 2024 to inspect illegal cellar construction.

statement: Scheme to Defraud (§190.65)
legal_significance: Criminal charge. Five-phase scheme combining illegal cellar conversion, concealment, evidence tampering, perjured affidavit, and extortion demand.

statement: Violations of Rules of Professional Conduct 3.3 (candor toward tribunal), 8.4(c) (dishonesty/fraud), and 8.4(d) (conduct prejudicial to administration of justice)
legal_significance: Disciplinary charges referred against Giacovas and associated counsel (Felix, Judell). Presentment of perjured affidavit to court and concealment of cellar illegal use.

statement: Michael Bleich does not own, has never owned, and has no legal claim to any HVAC equipment on the roof of 13 Harrison Street
legal_significance: HVAC ownership fabrication

statement: Every piece of HVAC equipment on the roof and bulkhead of 13 Harrison Street connects exclusively to Unit 2's mechanical systems and is owned by Unit 2
legal_significance: Equipment ownership and connection verification

statement: Bleich manufactured ownership of equipment that belongs to his neighbor, swore to it under oath, obtained a court order based on that lie, and used the order to destroy his neighbor's property while demanding millions of dollars to go away
legal_significance: Perjury, fraudulent court order, property destruction, extortion scheme

statement: The rooftop HVAC at 13 Harrison Street consists of condensing units installed on the bulkhead roof—a structure that is, under the Condominium Declaration, Article 6, Section B(1), a Limited Common Element to be exclusively used and maintained by the Owner(s) of Unit #2
legal_significance: Title/ownership status of bulkhead roof

statement: Unit 1's access to the roof is strictly restricted to structural repairs and fire emergencies
legal_significance: Unit 1 roof access restrictions

statement: An annotated mechanical schematic in the record explicitly states: Previously incorrectly identified as Unit 1's HVAC
legal_significance: Equipment misidentification acknowledgment

statement: The electrical disconnect box for the disputed equipment was photographed in the OFF position—it had been off
legal_significance: Equipment non-functionality evidence

statement: The refrigerant lines, electrical connections, and ductwork all trace back to Unit 2's mechanical systems. The equipment the Bleichs claimed as theirs in court actually serves Unit 2
legal_significance: Physical connections prove Unit 2 ownership

statement: In November 2025, Bleich's own MEP technicians from NY Brownstone LLC—Ricardo Singh and Josue Peña—made a devastating discovery during their inspection: they had been maintaining the wrong unit throughout the entire year
legal_significance: Own technicians discovered equipment misidentification

statement: The equipment Bleich had been paying to service was not his. It belonged to Unit 2
legal_significance: Equipment ownership conclusion from technician discovery

statement: This is a man who went to court, swore under oath that specific equipment was his sole source of heating, obtained an injunction to protect it, and then had his own technicians discover it was someone else's machine
legal_significance: Perjury in court proceedings

statement: Unit 1's actual HVAC infrastructure consists of three completely separate systems, none of which are on the roof: A gas-fired boiler in the cellar, a rear-yard condensing unit, and wall-mounted supplemental units
legal_significance: Unit 1's actual HVAC systems

statement: The 1995 Certificate of Occupancy confirms a Boiler Room in Unit 1's cellar
legal_significance: Unit 1 heating system verification

statement: Hydronic baseboard radiators throughout Unit 1 connect to this boiler. This is Unit 1's primary heating system and always has been
legal_significance: Unit 1 primary heating confirmation

statement: Unit 1's primary cooling is provided by a condensing unit located in the rear yard/backyard, entirely separate from any rooftop equipment
legal_significance: Unit 1 cooling system location

statement: Morgan Brill confirmed this in her correspondence: Mike put his HVAC on the coping wall outside the window of the pink bedroom
legal_significance: Unit 1 rear-yard condenser unit

statement: Bleich himself admitted to Irene Vogt on February 3, 2025, that he supplements with other wall units
legal_significance: Supplemental HVAC units admission

statement: Published listing photographs show window AC units in the cellar bedrooms—the very rooms Bleich swore were heated by the rooftop system
legal_significance: Supplemental cooling in cellar bedrooms; contradiction with court testimony

statement: Bleich has three independent HVAC systems, none on the roof. He swore to the court that a rooftop system that belongs to someone else was his sole source of heating
legal_significance: Perjury summary—false testimony about sole heating source

statement: Abdallah Elcherbini, AIA (EC Architects, License No. 044238): Unit 1 has a gas boiler in the cellar as its primary heating source, a rear-yard condensing unit for cooling, and that the rooftop HVAC units have no legal standing as they were never signed off under DOB applications
legal_significance: Expert confirmation of Unit 1 actual systems and rooftop illegality

statement: The Daikin heat pump on the main roof appears to be non-functional
legal_significance: Equipment functionality assessment

statement: The units encroach on the neighboring property line in violation of zoning ordinances
legal_significance: Zoning violation allegation

statement: James Powers, P.E. (JP Engineering Consultants): confirmed the non-functional status of the rooftop equipment, the banned R-410A refrigerant, the misidentification of CU-3 (which Bleich claimed as his but which serves Unit 2), and multiple code violations
legal_significance: Expert confirmation of equipment defects and code violations

statement: Irene Vogt (Unit 2 resident, sworn affirmation March 2, 2026): neither rooftop unit was working, that Bleich supplements with wall units, and that Unit 2 went through two full summers without rooftop AC because the injunction prevented installation of Unit 2's own replacement system
legal_significance: Eyewitness testimony to non-functionality and injunction harm

statement: The rooftop equipment uses Daikin R-410A refrigerant. Under the EPA's AIM Act and the Kigali Amendment to the Montreal Protocol, the manufacture of new HVAC equipment using R-410A was effectively prohibited as of January 1, 2025. The existing units cannot be legally repaired or replaced in kind
asserted_in: EPA AIM Act; Kigali Amendment to Montreal Protocol
legal_significance: Legal prohibition on equipment repair/replacement

statement: The Court is being asked to protect a machine that is: (a) unpermitted, (b) broken, (c) redundant to Unit 1's actual systems, (d) illegally sited on a Limited Common Element, (e) connected to Unit 2's systems rather than Unit 1's, (f) served illegal cellar bedrooms, and (g) uses a banned refrigerant
legal_significance: Comprehensive disqualification of injunction request

statement: This is not irreparable harm. This is an absurdity preserved by perjury
legal_significance: Conclusion on injunction basis

statement: In all the filings, affirmations, and memoranda Bleich and his counsel have submitted to the court, they have never produced a single document establishing Bleich's ownership of rooftop HVAC equipment. No purchase receipt. No installation contract. No DOB permit. No maintenance agreement identifying the equipment as Unit 1's. No engineering diagram showing connections to Unit 1. No Board authorization for installation on Unit 2's Limited Common Element
legal_significance: Complete absence of documentary proof

statement: The equipment is not his. It never was. And when his own technicians inspected it in November 2025 and discovered they had been maintaining the wrong unit, the fraud was laid bare. But by then, the injunction had been in place for ten months, the building had sustained 299 documented failure points, and Bleich had already sent his $6–8 million extortion demand
legal_significance: Fraud timeline and damages

statement: The case is framed not as perjury in a condo case but as a $22 million extortion scheme using a court order obtained through perjury—because that is precisely what the evidence establishes
asserted_by: Litigation team, Adam Leitman Bailey, P.C.
legal_significance: Criminal prosecution framing

statement: Giacovas verified and submitted pleadings containing known falsehoods and filed them with the County Clerk
legal_significance: Attorney misconduct allegation

statement: His Medifast sanctions history—where a federal court found he fabricated quotes for maximum shock value and ordered $198,877.50 in fees—establishes a career pattern
legal_significance: Attorney prior misconduct pattern

statement: The Term Sheet §5(a) suggests participation in the extortion scheme itself

statement: Her NJ Pro Hac Vice status was confirmed ineligible

statement: Vacate the preliminary injunction immediately under CPLR §6314 and §5015(a)(3), as an injunction obtained through fraud must be dissolved. Every material factual representation supporting 'irreparable harm' was willfully false. The equipment is not Bleich's. It does not work. It was never his sole source of heating. The injunction protects a machine that belongs to someone else.

statement: Grand Larceny by Extortion (2nd Degree) under PL §155.40(2), Class C Felony: The extortion premium of $1.1–5.5 million (above Bleich's own valuation) vastly exceeds the $50,000 threshold. The use of a court order as the instrument of coercion is an aggravating factor.
legal_significance: Lead criminal charge for prosecution with aggravating factors identified

statement: Perjury in the First Degree, PL §210.15, Class D Felony (three counts): Each count carries a maximum of 2½–7 years. Multiple acts of perjury in the same proceeding as part of a systematic fraud may support consecutive sentencing; however, New York courts typically impose concurrent sentences for related perjury counts.
legal_significance: Secondary criminal charge for prosecution; three separate counts identified

statement: Tampering with Physical Evidence, PL §215.40(2), Class E Felony: The sheetrock concealment—captured on video—is physical proof of consciousness of guilt. This charge is independent of the perjury and may be sentenced consecutively.
legal_significance: Tertiary criminal charge for prosecution; video evidence of tampering; consecutive sentencing available

statement: Scheme to Defraud (1st Degree), PL §190.65, Class E Felony: Overlaps with the extortion charge but captures the systematic nature of the scheme.
legal_significance: Quaternary criminal charge for prosecution; overlaps with extortion but separately captures systematic nature

statement: Grand Larceny by Extortion (2nd Degree)—PL §155.40(2)—Class C Felony: For a first-time felony offender with no prior criminal conviction, the New York Penal Law provides a sentencing range of 1 to 15 years. Given the sophistication of the scheme, the dollar amounts, and the FINRA pattern, a conviction would likely result in a sentence of 3–7 years of incarceration.
legal_significance: Predicted sentencing range for lead charge based on statutory minimums, sophistication, and aggravating factors

statement: Perjury in the First Degree—PL §210.15—Class D Felony (three counts): Predicted: 2–4 years per count, served concurrently with the extortion sentence.
legal_significance: Predicted sentencing range for perjury counts; concurrent with extortion sentence

statement: Tampering with Physical Evidence—PL §215.40(2)—Class E Felony: Predicted: 1–2 years, potentially consecutive.
legal_significance: Predicted sentencing range for tampering charge; potentially consecutive to other sentences

statement: Scheme to Defraud (1st Degree)—PL §190.65—Class E Felony: Predicted: 1–3 years, concurrent.
legal_significance: Predicted sentencing range for defraud charge; concurrent with other sentences

statement: Aggregate predicted sentence: 4–8 years of incarceration, with the possibility of supervised release after the minimum. For a defendant with no prior criminal record, New York courts may consider alternatives to incarceration for the lesser charges. However, the Grand Larceny by Extortion charge would likely require state prison time.
legal_significance: Overall sentencing prediction combining all counts with consideration of defendant's criminal history and statutory factors

statement: The FINRA $3 million sanction and permanent bar are civil regulatory actions, not criminal convictions, but are admissible at sentencing as evidence of the defendant's character and propensity for systematic regulatory fraud.
asserted_in: Section IV: The FINRA Aggravating Factor
legal_significance: Evidentiary basis for sentencing enhancement; demonstrates pattern and propensity

statement: The pattern is identical across both contexts: identify a structural vulnerability in the system (settlement loopholes on Wall Street; injunction mechanics in Supreme Court), exploit it ruthlessly for financial gain, shift consequences onto victims, and treat any penalty as a transaction cost.
asserted_in: Section IV: The FINRA Aggravating Factor
legal_significance: Character assessment and pattern evidence showing systematic approach to fraud across regulatory contexts

statement: Bleich created his own valuation spreadsheet showing $4.7–4.9 million, then demanded $6–8 million in the same email. No expert testimony needed. The jury can read it.

statement: The sheetrock concealment video is a separate crime (PL §215.40) that proves consciousness of guilt. A grand jury can watch it in 30 seconds.

legal_significance: Physical evidence demonstrating consciousness of guilt through documented tampering

statement: Destruction of the 'Residential Necessity' Predicate. Post-injunction technician findings confirm neither rooftop unit was functional.
legal_significance: Defense Pillar 1

statement: Changed Circumstances (CPLR §6314). The boiler discovery, rear-yard condenser, and dead equipment collectively eliminate factual basis for injunction.
legal_significance: Defense Pillar 2

statement: Documentary Proof of Extortionate Purpose. Term Sheet §5(a), $6–8M demand, and self-contradicting valuation spreadsheet prove injunction serves coercion.
legal_significance: Defense Pillar 3

statement: Board Compliance Resolutions. Board Resolutions 2026-01 through 2026-05 demonstrate full good-faith compliance with Court's cooperation directive.
legal_significance: Defense Pillar 4

statement: Fee-Shifting Weapons. 22 NYCRR §130-1.1, Judiciary Law §487, Anti-SLAPP create financial consequences for continued prosecution.
legal_significance: Defense Pillar 5

statement: CLAIM: Valid Board Meeting on Oct. 15, 2018 adopted ROFR waiver | FACT: No in-person meeting occurred. Brill was never notified. No quorum existed.
legal_significance: Documented material misstatement

statement: CLAIM: Brill resigned before Oct. 15, leaving Board 'duly constituted' | FACT: Brill resigned effective Oct. 18. She was co-President through the meeting date.
legal_significance: Documented material misstatement

statement: CLAIM: Bleich and Brill were 'friends' with shared building vision | FACT: Met in person once or twice. 'Distant, inconsiderate and unhelpful.'
legal_significance: Documented material misstatement

statement: CLAIM: Had 'agreement' for 60/40 split of building sale proceeds | FACT: 'Definitely not true.' Brill: his insistence on this was her 'primary reason to get the hell out of Dodge.'
legal_significance: Documented material misstatement

statement: CLAIM: Unit 2 owner 'interfered' with $3.4M 'bona fide offer' | FACT: Kornmeier testified BLEICH REFUSED the $3.4M offer. No third-party interference.
legal_significance: Documented material misstatement

statement: CLAIM: 'Damages' of no less than $3.9M from lost sale | FACT: Unit appraised at $2.5M. The 'damage' exceeds total unit value by 56%.
legal_significance: Documented material misstatement

statement: CLAIM: Unit 2 owner planned to convert building to 'commercial use' | FACT: No DOB filings exist. BLEICH'S OWN 12/1/2018 email discusses 'conversion to office or retail' for HIS unit.
legal_significance: Documented material misstatement

statement: CLAIM: Unit 2 owner 'removed' Bleich and 'seized' the Board | FACT: Bleich changed bylaws in secret, excluded 75% owner, refused meetings. Court ruled for Unit 2 owner.
legal_significance: Documented material misstatement

statement: CLAIM: Roof HVAC 'provides heat to our home' and is 'necessary and critical' | FACT: Unit 1 has gas-fired boiler + hydronic radiators as primary heat. Roof HVAC confirmed non-functional Nov. 2025.
legal_significance: Documented material misstatement

statement: CLAIM: 'Board has not provided any alternatives to providing heating' | FACT: Unit 1 has boiler, radiators, rear-yard condenser. Bleich admitted he 'supplements with other wall units.'
legal_significance: Documented material misstatement

statement: CLAIM: HVAC 'installed ~17 years ago' with implied longstanding reliance | FACT: No DOB permit exists. Longevity proves 17 years of knowing violation, not legitimacy.
legal_significance: Documented material misstatement

statement: CLAIM: HVAC serves 'Plaintiffs' residential unit' (implying lawful dwelling) | FACT: HVAC primarily served illegal cellar bedrooms. C of O restricts cellar to 'Rec Room, Storage, Meters.' Giacovas admitted cellar bedroom use on 9/15/2022.
legal_significance: Documented material misstatement

statement: CLAIM: Removal would cause 'irreparable harm' | FACT: Both rooftop units confirmed NON-FUNCTIONAL (Nov. 2025). Cannot be 'irreparable' to remove dead equipment.
legal_significance: Documented material misstatement

statement: CLAIM: Unit 1 'owns' rooftop HVAC and has 'express easement' | FACT: All rooftop/bulkhead equipment connects to Unit 2's systems. Annotated schematic: 'Previously incorrectly identified as Unit 1's HVAC.'
legal_significance: Documented material misstatement

statement: CLAIM: Cellar used in compliance with C of O | FACT: Used as bedrooms for 3 children. Marketing labels rooms 'SLEEPING AREA.' Video shows sheetrock concealment before DOB inspection.
legal_significance: Documented material misstatement

statement: CLAIM: Stealth filing: listed represented defendants as unrepresented; did not disclose pending 2019 action | FACT: Defendants had existing counsel (Berry at CLM). 2019 action actively pending with status conference scheduled.
legal_significance: Documented material misstatement

statement: CLAIM: Unit 1 worth $6–8M (May 2025 demand) | FACT: Bleich's OWN spreadsheet: $4.7–4.9M. Bowery: $2.5M. Highest offer ever: $3.4M (which Bleich himself refused).
legal_significance: Documented material misstatement

statement: CLAIM: 50-50 split of building proceeds is 'appropriate' | FACT: Bleich holds 25% common interest. Declaration and By-Laws establish 75/25 split.
legal_significance: Documented material misstatement

statement: CLAIM: Unit 1 is 'in top condition' | FACT: Illegal cellar bedrooms; non-functional HVAC; unpermitted equipment; C of O violations; $16,663.71 in unpaid arrears.
legal_significance: Documented material misstatement

statement: CLAIM: Term Sheet §5(a): Injunction 'shall remain in effect' during settlement | FACT: Uses perjury-obtained injunction as permanent coercive leverage while water destroys Unit 2.
legal_significance: Documented material misstatement

statement: CLAIM: 'Strong interest at 3.4' (April 2021) justified not selling | FACT: No transaction ever closed at any price. Bleich later demanded $6–8M—nearly triple the 'interest' price.
legal_significance: Documented material misstatement

statement: CLAIM: DOB complaints and regulatory harassment were 'legitimate' | FACT: Multiple complaints resolved 'no violation warranted.' Counsel actively lobbied DOB to reject permits.
legal_significance: Documented material misstatement

statement: CLAIM: Bleich 'cooperated' with replacement HVAC solution | FACT: Systematically obstructed every proposal: denied alternatives existed (Mar. 2025), then acknowledged yard could work (Jul. 2025), then delayed specifications, then refused.
legal_significance: Documented material misstatement

statement: CLAIM: Defendants engaged in 'service gamesmanship' (acknowledged by Court) | FACT: Bleich attempted to evade service under the Hague Convention through procedural stalling.
legal_significance: Documented material misstatement

statement: Grand Larceny by Extortion (2nd Degree) - PL §155.40(2)
legal_significance: Class C Felony, max 5–15 years. Evidence: $6–8M demand vs. $2.5M appraisal; Term Sheet §5(a) weaponizing injunction

statement: Perjury 1st Degree (Count 1) - PL §210.15
legal_significance: Class D Felony, max 2½–7 years. Evidence: HVAC 'provides heating' — all rooftop equipment connects to Unit 2, not Unit 1

statement: Perjury 1st Degree (Count 2) - PL §210.15
legal_significance: Class D Felony, max 2½–7 years. Evidence: 'necessary and critical' — boiler, radiators, rear-yard condenser all concealed

statement: Perjury 1st Degree (Count 3) - PL §210.15
legal_significance: Class D Felony, max 2½–7 years. Evidence: 'no other common area' — rear-yard location acknowledged 7/23/2025

statement: Offering False Instrument (1st Degree) - PL §175.35
legal_significance: Class E Felony, max 1½–4 years. Evidence: Filed verified complaint with known falsehoods via NYSCEF to County Clerk

statement: Tampering with Physical Evidence - PL §215.40(2)
legal_significance: Class E Felony, max 1½–4 years. Evidence: Sheetrock walls over illegal cellar bathrooms before DOB inspection; video evidence

statement: Obstruction of Govt. Admin. (2nd Degree) - PL §195.05
legal_significance: Class A Misdemeanor, max 1 year. Evidence: Concealment prevented DOB from documenting illegal conversion; 5 refused inspections

statement: Scheme to Defraud (1st Degree) - PL §190.65
legal_significance: Class E Felony, max 1½–4 years. Evidence: Systematic scheme 2024–2026: perjury → injunction → destruction → extortion → Term Sheet

statement: Criminal Mischief (3rd Degree) - PL §145.05
legal_significance: Class E Felony, max 1½–4 years. Evidence: Maintained perjury-obtained injunction causing $2.2M+ physical damage to Unit 2
